picks.reasoning: Detroit is 52-19 with NetRtg +7.8 vs Atlanta's +1.8 — a 6.0-point gap that crosses the meaningful threshold. Detroit is home, which adds ~2-3 points of edge. CRITICAL: Cade Cunningham is confirmed OUT (Pneumothorax) per official NBA injury report — this is their franchise player. However, at 52-19 with 7 rotation players available, the Pistons have sufficient depth. Atlanta is L10 9-1 but overall 40-32 (.556 W%) — the hot streak fade rule applies: sub-.600 true talent suggests regression, though their recent form is hot. Atlanta's NetRtg +1.8 is materially weaker than Detroit's +7.8. Franchise player penalty for Cunningham OUT: base confidence starts at 72 (strong home team, NetRtg gap, opponent regression candidate), apply -15 for franchise player OUT = 57, then note opponent also doesn't have a star absent as direct offset. However, Detroit has been winning without Cunningham already this season (52-19 record suggests team is functional). Confidence settles at 62. EV = (0.62 × 1.70) - 1 = 1.054 - 1 = 0.054. EV = 5.4% — marginal but clears 5% floor. Line anomaly check: Detroit home at 1.70 is the clear favourite — no anomaly. Spread: Detroit -2.5 at 1.88 is also in range but ML at 1.70 just at bottom of target range; with Cunningham OUT confidence in spread is below 60 floor, so ML only. Stake: confidence 62 = medium tier (55-69) = 15% of bankroll = €115.53.

Cleveland is 45-27 (L10: 7-3, NetRtg +4.3) hosting Miami (38-34, L10: 5-5, NetRtg +2.5). NetRtg gap = 1.8 — below the 4.0 threshold for a strong signal, but Cleveland has home court (+2-3 pts effective advantage) and a better record. Miami has a 4-game losing streak (L4) which is a motivation flag. Miami's injury report shows Terry Rozier OUT (Not With Team) which is significant for their guard rotation. Rozier is not a franchise player but is a key rotation piece. Cleveland has Jarrett Allen OUT (confirmed), Craig Porter Jr. OUT, Tyrese Proctor OUT — roster-depleted but none are franchise players. Cleveland's OffRtg 119.0 is solid; Miami OffRtg 115.8. DefRtg gap: Cleveland 114.7 vs Miami 113.3 — slight Miami edge on defence but not >8 pts significant. Home court + better record + Miami skid + Rozier absent. Base confidence: 65. Apply -5 for close NetRtg gap (below 4.0 threshold, home court partially compensates): confidence 63. ML odds 1.68 — this is BELOW the 1.70 target floor. FLAGGING: The odds at 1.68 fall just outside the 1.80–2.50 ML target range. Per skills: Commit confirmation floor is 1.65 for already-validated picks. Since this is a Scout draft (not confirmed), the 1.70 floor applies. 1.68 < 1.70 — this pick does NOT meet the ML odds target. REJECTING this pick on odds grounds. Recalculate: EV = (0.63 × 1.68) - 1 = 1.058 - 1 = 0.058, EV passes, but odds target fails. Per market_rules, ML odds target 1.70–2.50. Do not draft. DROPPING this pick.

Since Cleveland ML at 1.68 is below odds target, evaluating the spread. Cleveland -3.0 at 1.90 is in the 1.80–2.30 spread target range. Cleveland's home advantage, better record (45-27 vs 38-34), L10 advantage (7-3 vs 5-5), NetRtg advantage (+4.3 vs +2.5). Miami on a 4-game skid; Rozier OUT. Cleveland at home with NetRtg +4.3 should cover a 3-point spread. No B2B issues for either team tonight. No extreme injury concerns on the franchise player level (Cleveland's absences are role players). Spread confidence floor is 60. Game-time decisions: Dosunmu (Questionable) for Minnesota not relevant; for THIS game, no significant GTD concerns flagged beyond Jalen Johnson (Atlanta — different game). For this Cleveland-Miami game: no game-time decision concerns flagged for either team beyond Rozier already OUT. Confidence: 60 (exactly at floor). EV = (0.60 × 1.90) - 1 = 1.14 - 1 = 0.14. EV = 14% — solid marginal pick. Stake: confidence 60 = medium tier low end = 15% = €115.53. This replaces the rejected ML pick for Cleveland.

Effective adjusted gap: ~0.6-1.6 pts in OKC's favour — slim but OKC is clearly the superior team. Boston injuries: Nikola Vucevic OUT (Fracture), confirmed. Celtics G League absences only beyond Vucevic. Star rest risk for OKC: skills note rest risk increasing with postseason clinch imminent. OKC at 57-15 with clinch likely done — star rest is a real concern. However, road game at Boston (rivalry, playoff-seeding opponent) makes rest less likely. Line anomaly: OKC road at 1.71 as favourite vs Boston home at 2.16 — OKC being priced as road favourite is unusual but justified by their dominance (11.2 NetRtg vs 7.6). No anomaly flag triggered. LATE SEASON: OKC locked into top seed — motivation to rest stars is elevated but road game at top-5 team argues against rest. Net assessment: OKC should win this game. Confidence 68 (strong team, slight NetRtg gap, road disadvantage factored, slight star rest risk discount from 72). EV = (0.68 × 1.71) - 1 = 1.163 - 1 = 0.163. EV = 16.3% — strong edge. Odds 1.71 just clears the 1.70 ML floor. Stake: confidence 68 = medium tier high end = 20% × €770.17 = €154.03. However, noting this is a road pick against a 47-24 team — capping at 15% = €115.53 given home court and near-threshold NetRtg gap.

Slate quality: Moderate — four picks identified with genuine edge. The OKC road pick is the standout at EV 16.3%. Several games were degraded by key injuries (Giannis, Kyrie, Curry, Edwards, Embiid) making lines difficult to evaluate cleanly. Total exposure: €462.12 (~60% of bankroll), within the 70% daily cap.

bets.reasoning: Cleveland (45-27, NetRtg +4.3) hosts Miami (38-34, L10 5-5, NetRtg +2.5). Terry Rozier confirmed OUT (Not With Team). Jarrett Allen OUT for Cleveland already priced in. Critically, Scout's other Cleveland absences (Craig Porter Jr., Tyrese Proctor) do NOT appear on tonight's official injury report, suggesting those players are available — a positive upgrade for Cleveland (+5 confidence). Miami on a 4-game losing skid. Spread moved from -3.0 to -2.5 in our favour (easier line to cover). Cleveland's home court, better record, NetRtg advantage, and Miami's depleted guard rotation support covering 2.5 points. No B2B concerns for either team. Confidence 65 (up from 60 due to Cleveland roster upgrade). EV = (0.65 × 1.90) - 1 = 0.235 (23.5%). Odds at 1.90 within 1.80–2.30 spread target range.

bets.match: Boston Celtics vs Oklahoma City Thunder
bets.reasoning: OKC (57-15, L10 10-0, NetRtg +11.2 — best in the league) at Boston (47-24, L10 6-4, NetRtg +7.6). OKC road favourites priced at 1.79 (improved from Scout's 1.71 — movement in our favour). Jalen Williams confirmed AVAILABLE (not on tonight's official injury report). Boston missing Nikola Vucevic (OUT, fracture) — already priced in. OKC's only absentees are G League assignments (Barnhizer, Sandfort, Topic, Sorber). NetRtg gap of +3.6 pts in OKC's favour, with home court reducing effective gap to ~0.6–1.6 pts, but OKC's dominance (11.2 NetRtg, 11-game win streak) justifies road favourite pricing. Star rest risk minimal — road game at top-5 team (playoff seeding implications). Odds improved +0.08 at commit; confirming at original stake per line movement rules. EV = (0.68 × 1.79) - 1 = 0.217 (21.7%). Odds 1.79 within 1.70–2.50 ML target range.
anchor_players: Nikola Vucevic (Boston — OUT, Fracture, priced in)

Line moved 0.25 against our pick (Scout: 2.02 → Commit: 1.77 for Houston away ML). Movement > 0.20 against triggers major signal review. Per line_movement_rules: apply confidence -10. Adjusted confidence: 55 - 10 = 45 — below the 50 minimum threshold. EV at adjusted confidence: (0.45 × 1.77) - 1 = -0.204, deeply negative. Cancelled: line moved > 0.20 against with confidence falling below minimum after mandatory adjustment.
